desc: Phoenix combines Sonoran Desert landscapes, winter-sun comfort, and big-city dining/sports infrastructure, with the key trade-off being extreme summer heat versus premium spring/autumn demand.
hls: Prime base for desert hiking, cacti landscapes, and golf-season travel in cooler months.
Best outdoor comfort usually runs Nov–Apr; May–Sep often requires heat-aware scheduling.
Major event calendar (MLB spring training, sports weekends, festivals) can push short-notice rates up.
Easy add-ons include Scottsdale, Tempe, Sedona day/overnight extensions, and Grand Canyon loops.
Strong urban food scene with Southwestern, Mexican, and contemporary US dining variety.
Car-centric layout means itinerary efficiency improves with a rental vehicle.

todo.name: Desert Botanical Garden + Papago Park
todo.desc: Pair cactus-collection gardens with short red-rock walks and sunset viewpoints in Papago Park.

todo.name: Camelback or Piestewa sunrise hike
todo.desc: Start at first light on marked routes to avoid afternoon heat and secure parking.

todo.name: Roosevelt Row arts + downtown dining
todo.desc: Spend an evening around murals, galleries, and chef-led restaurants in central Phoenix.

todo.name: Scottsdale Old Town circuit
todo.desc: Combine galleries, boutiques, and Sonoran-influenced food stops in a walkable district.

todo.name: Taliesin West or Heard Museum
todo.desc: Choose one signature culture stop focused on Frank Lloyd Wright design or Native art history.

todo.name: Day trip to Sedona
todo.desc: Use one full day for red-rock viewpoints and short hikes, leaving early for cooler trail windows.

wifi.note: Very good in hotels/coworking venues; mobile coverage is strong across metro Phoenix.

prac.fltNote: London to Phoenix is usually one-stop (commonly via Dallas, Chicago, LA, or other US hubs). Return fares are often ~£480–£760 off-peak, ~£760–£1,050 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,050+ around spring-training, Thanksgiving, and Christmas travel spikes.
prac.currency: US Dollar (USD); card acceptance is near-universal, with tap-to-pay common.
prac.lang: English is primary; Spanish is widely used across hospitality and local services.
prac.tz: UTC-07:00 (Arizona, no DST; time differs seasonally from many US states).
prac.visa: For the United States, eligible travelers must secure ESTA under the Visa Waiver Program, while other nationalities need a U.S. visitor visa before arrival.
alerts: Summer heat can be dangerous—plan outdoor activity at dawn and carry more water than usual.
Rental-car demand and hotel prices jump during MLB spring training and major event weekends.
Trail closures or restrictions can occur on extreme-heat days.

sweet: Late November to April is typically the strongest comfort window for outdoor plans; for better value, target late April or October and schedule hikes early.
